summary.intent: Merge main into the httpd-crate branch, resolve conflicts, fix code review findings from greptile on PR #296 and PR #465, and maintain a clean passing CI.
summary.outcome: All merges completed and pushed; greptile findings across two PRs fixed; a git history rewrite (removing logs.txt) forced creation of a new PR (#465); E2E sandbox test failures identified as pre-existing/environmental, not caused by branch changes.

repo: TLS (rustls) was refactored from moltis-gateway into moltis-httpd; rustls crypto provider install_default() must be called in prepare_gateway (not start_gateway) so both the standard and embedded startup paths initialize TLS.

Browser warmup tool (browser_tool_for_warmup) was being silently dropped by a destructure pattern (..) in PreparedGatewayCore; it must be explicitly extracted and threaded through BannerMeta to reach start_browser_warmup_after_listener.
proxy_shutdown_tx was being dropped by the .. destructure pattern, which would kill the trusted-network proxy prematurely; it must be stored in PreparedGateway.
Session event enrichment (label, model, preview, channel binding) was lost during the gateway→httpd migration and had to be ported manually.
The /ws route alias must exist alongside /ws/chat for node-add generated commands to work.
release.yml had 4 redundant pre-version build steps (one per job: Linux pkg, AppImage, macOS/generic, Windows) that caused unnecessary double-compilation.
The sandbox E2E tests (Refresh button) appear to be pre-existing failures tied to Docker/container runtime availability in the test environment, not caused by branch changes.
cron sessions should show a delete button (same as non-main sessions); the merge conflict resolution incorrectly added isCron to the hide condition.

code.finding: install_default() for rustls must live in prepare_gateway, not start_gateway, to cover both the swift-bridge/embedded and normal startup paths.
Launchd plist values need xml_escape to prevent malformed XML from special chars in URLs/names; systemd ExecStart arguments need quoting to handle spaces in display names and working directories.
Duplicate /api/sessions route registrations existed and would cause an axum panic at startup.
Delete button visibility condition was incorrectly set to !(isMain || isCron) during merge conflict resolution; should be !isMain so cron sessions remain deletable.
Local MAX_LIFETIME constant was shadowed by module-level MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME and unused.
PreparedGatewayCore fields needed to be pub for httpd crate to access them without a gateway dependency.

workflow: git filter-repo removes the 'origin' remote as a safety measure after rewriting history; it must be re-added manually before pushing.
Force-pushing a rewritten history to GitHub closes any open PRs on that branch because commit SHAs no longer match; a new PR must be created.

After git filter-repo rewrites the full history, gh pr create fails with 'no common ancestor' if the branch was created against the rewritten base; the fix is to create a new branch from origin/main, apply the diff, and force-push.
nightly rustfmt requires grouped use blocks (use { crate_a::{...}, crate_b::{...} }) rather than separate use statements for items from different crates.
greptile review threads must be resolved via the GitHub API after fixing the underlying code; they do not auto-resolve on push.

friction: git filter-repo rewrote the entire repo history (all 2027 commits) rather than just branch commits, breaking the shared ancestor with origin/main and closing PR #296.
Recreating the PR after history rewrite required creating a new branch from origin/main and applying the diff as a squash commit, losing per-commit granularity.
gh CLI had no default remote set after filter-repo removed origin, causing gh pr reopen to fail.
The upstream tracking branch was set to origin/main after force-push, requiring an explicit git push origin HEAD to fix.
Multiple rounds of greptile comments across two PRs required repeated fetch/fix/resolve cycles.
moltis-agents was not a dependency of moltis-httpd, requiring a Cargo.toml edit to thread AgentTool through the httpd server startup path.
23 merge conflicts in the first merge required parallel agent-based resolution.
open_items: Sandbox E2E tests (Refresh button, clean-all endpoint) are failing in the test environment; root cause (Docker/container runtime availability) not fixed, just confirmed as pre-existing.
The squash commit approach for PR #465 loses the individual commit history from the original branch work.
The Onboarding Anthropic provider E2E test ('continue without selecting a model still persists Anthropic credentials') failed but was not investigated.
